questions.question: What is a stack data structure?
options: A) A collection of elements in a Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) order
B) A collection of elements in a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) order
C) A collection of elements in a Random Access order
D) A collection of elements in a Linked List order
questions.explanation: A stack follows the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) principle, meaning the last element added to the stack will be the first one to be removed.

questions.question: What is the 'Push' operation in a stack?
options: A) To add an element to the top of the stack
B) To remove an element from the top of the stack
C) To remove an element from the bottom of the stack
D) To add an element to the bottom of the stack
questions.explanation: In a stack, the 'Push' operation involves adding an element to the top of the stack.

questions.question: Which data structure is commonly used to implement a stack?
options: A) Array
B) Linked List
C) Tree
D) Graph
questions.explanation: Arrays are commonly used to implement stacks due to their efficient 'Push' and 'Pop' operations.

questions.question: What is the 'Pop' operation in a stack?
options: A) To add an element to the top of the stack
B) To remove an element from the top of the stack
C) To remove an element from the bottom of the stack
D) To add an element to the bottom of the stack
questions.explanation: In a stack, the 'Pop' operation involves removing an element from the top of the stack.

questions.question: Applications of stacks include?
options: A) Database query optimization
B) Evaluating postfix expressions
C) Implementing recursive algorithms
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Stacks have various applications, including database query optimization, evaluating postfix expressions, and implementing recursive algorithms.
